title: Amperos Health secures $16M in series A funding, unveils AI-native denial management solution
summary: Amperos Health CEO and co-founder Michal Miernowski told Fierce Healthcare the newly announced funding will support scaling the organization and its platform.

title: Oak HC/FT backs Courier Health's $50M series B to build out AI for the biopharma patient experience
summary: Courier Health raised $50 million in series B funding to scale its AI-powered platform purpose-built for biopharma that manages the end-to-end patient journey.

title: HCA Healthcare executives shrug off Q1 volumes disruptions, reaffirm FY2026 guidance
summary: A sharp end to the flu season and disruptive winter storms dragged the country's largest for-profit health system in January. Executives said there's already signs of recovery since the stumble and pointed to favorable activity among states' Medicaid subsidy programs.

title: Community Health Systems attributes Q1 volumes stumble to consumers' macroeconomic fears, payers' prior auth denials
summary: Same-store admissions and adjusted admissions respectively declined 1.3% and 0.5% year over year. Executives pointed to cross-market dropoffs among patients with high-deductible and managed care plans.

title: Amazon One Medical launches GLP-1 program integrated with primary care
summary: Patients can take advantage of the GLP-1 management program through their One Medical primary care clinician. Medications are available for quick delivery to all 50 states, with nearly 3,000 cities having same-day delivery available.

title: Healthcare finance leaders cite federal funding uncertainty, Medicaid cuts as top concerns
summary: Amid funding uncertainty, 57% of leaders report reducing costs as their top priority for the year—followed closely by managing strategic and performance improvement initiatives.

title: Flagging first-quarter volumes dragged CHS’ earnings
summary: Executives said consumer fears and aggressive denials from payers fueled declining volumes in the quarter, leading CHS to a $58 million loss. Still, the hospital operator expects volumes to pick up in the back half of the year.

title: FTC, US Anesthesia Partners reach settlement in Texas price collusion case
summary: The private equity-backed anesthesia provider bought and bullied its way to market dominance in Texas, driving up prices for patients, the FTC alleged. The parties have now reached a deal, though USAP will not admit liability.
